skill.name: security-engineering
skill.description: Security architecture and implementation guidance for authentication, authorization, threat modeling, compliance controls, secure development, and incident response planning.
skill.summary: Design and review security controls for applications, infrastructure, identity, compliance, and incident response.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, C2, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation-only security guidance in SKILL.md. The external command hits are Markdown backticks and inline file references, and the high-risk keyword hits are security terminology without executable code, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences and inline reference formatting. They do not execute commands and do not define scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or inline file names in documentation. No interpreter, script file, command invocation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Acronym Reported as C2 Keyword
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword hit appears inside the compliance acronym SOC2. It describes a compliance framework, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line lists SOC2 with HIPAA and PCI-DSS as compliance controls. There is no network endpoint, beaconing logic, or operational C2 instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Terminology Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are security education terms and table entries. No weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or ECB mode is recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content discusses secure controls, misconfiguration, deserialization, and incident severity tables. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm usage or instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Defensive Review Terms Reported as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits appear in defensive secure development and secrets management guidance. They do not instruct system discovery or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines advise avoiding untrusted deserialization and auditing secret access. No host enumeration, port scanning, or data collection workflow is present.

content.user_title: Design Secure Systems with Proven Controls
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent security guidance across architecture, identity, development, compliance, and incident response. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured security patterns for planning and review.
actual_capabilities: Explains defense-in-depth and zero trust security architecture patterns.
Guides OAuth 2.0, OIDC, session management, and MFA decisions.
Compares RBAC, ABAC, and policy-as-code authorization approaches.
Maps secure development practices to OWASP Top 10 risk areas.
Summarizes SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and ISO 27001 control themes.
Outlines incident response phases and severity levels.
limitations: It provides guidance, not automated vulnerability scanning.
It does not include executable tools or test runners.
Referenced threat modeling and compliance files are not included in this package.
Framework details may need review against current legal or regulatory requirements.

use_cases.title: Review Application Security Design
use_cases.description: Use the skill to evaluate authentication, authorization, session handling, data protection, and logging choices before implementation.

use_cases.title: Plan Compliance Controls
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify common access, encryption, monitoring, vendor, and incident response controls for regulated systems.

use_cases.title: Prepare Incident Response Runbooks
use_cases.description: Use the skill to structure preparation, detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Basic Security Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a planned application
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this application design for core security controls. Focus on authentication, authorization, data protection, logging, and secure defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Authorization Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Selecting access control for a product
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare RBAC, ABAC, and policy-as-code for this product. Recommend a model and explain the operational tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Map Controls to Compliance Needs
prompt_templates.prompt: Map this system design to common SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and ISO 27001 control areas. Highlight gaps and evidence needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Security Review Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a security review plan for this release. Include threat modeling, OWASP risks, secrets management, monitoring, and incident response readiness.

output_examples.input: Review a web app that uses OAuth, role-based permissions, and centralized logging.
output_examples.output: The review would identify identity, authorization, session, logging, and data protection checks. It would also list likely gaps, such as token rotation, logout invalidation, and alert ownership.

output_examples.input: Prepare incident response severity levels for a SaaS platform.
output_examples.output: The response would define severity tiers, expected response times, containment steps, evidence handling, recovery checks, and post-incident review items.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether ABAC or RBAC fits a multi-tenant application.
output_examples.output: The response would compare policy complexity, auditability, implementation effort, tenant isolation, and change management before recommending a practical access model.

best_practices: Provide system context, data sensitivity, user roles, and compliance requirements before asking for security guidance.
Ask for prioritized risks and concrete control recommendations, not only general best practices.
Have a qualified security owner verify recommendations before production rollout.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as the only source for legal or regulatory compliance decisions.
Do not treat the guidance as proof that a system is secure.
Do not request offensive exploitation steps or instructions against real systems.

faq.question: Does this skill run security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides written security guidance and review structure, but it does not execute scanners or commands.

faq.question: Can it help with OAuth and OIDC decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers grant types, token lifetime, refresh tokens, revocation, and related session practices.

faq.question: Does it support compliance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It summarizes common control areas for SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and ISO 27001.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for a security engineer?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis, but expert review is still needed for high-risk systems.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide review prompts around OWASP risks, secrets handling, access control, and logging.

faq.question: Does it include the referenced files?
faq.answer: No. The package contains SKILL.md only, so referenced files should not be assumed available.
